indicators.name: large windows
indicators.rationale: Large windows allow natural light to enter, which can disrupt sleep patterns.

indicators.name: high ceilings
indicators.rationale: High ceilings can make the space feel cold and uninviting, potentially affecting sleep quality.

indicators.name: white walls
indicators.rationale: White walls can reflect light, creating a bright and stimulating environment.

indicators.name: lack of furniture
indicators.rationale: The absence of furniture makes the space feel sparse and unfurnished, which can be disorienting.

reasoning_summary: This indoor scene is not well-suited for primary sleep. The large windows, high ceilings, white walls, and lack of furniture all contribute to a bright, cold, and sparse environment that is incompatible with sleep. The score of 2 reflects the significant negative impact these features have on the suitability of the space for sleep.
